programmes.show_description: Intriguing mystery. Villagers in the south of France gather to witness a rare solar eclipse. But as the sun and moon converge, an injured girl inexplicably disappears. In French/subs. (Ep1)
Gordon Ramsay revisits three restaurants that he helped - Lido, Anna Vincenzo's and Le Bistro. Have the owners put his advice to good use?
Epic rags-to-riches drama, based on Barbara Taylor Bradford's bestseller, charting Emma Harte's rise from maid to mogul. With Brenda Blethyn, Jessica Reynolds and Emmett J. Scanlan. (Ep1/8)
Epic rags-to-riches drama with Brenda Blethyn, Jessica Reynolds and Emmett J. Scanlan. Emma and Edwin's forbidden love deepens, but can they ever overcome their class differences? (Ep2/8)
DIY queen Stacey Solomon helps homeowners salvage their half-built homes. Can she work her magic on Erick and Caroline's extension to bring their 'Japandi' vision back to life? (Ep1/6)
A new cellmate spells danger for Lucas, forcing Dillon to take desperate measures. Theresa pushes Mercedes to give Warren another chance. Diane and Tony lie to protect their loved ones.
Another chance to catch yesterday's new episode, with Nicki Chapman in Dictionary Corner. Colin Murray, Rachel Riley and Susie Dent host.
Ill-Gotten Gaines: Woody has Thanksgiving dinner with Kelly's family and realises that his father-in-law is having an affair. The rest of the gang celebrate at Cheers.
Feelings... Whoa, Whoa, Whoa: Carla's love-hate relationship with John Hill gets out of hand when a passionate encounter gives him a heart attack. Cliff has a new neighbour.
Daddy's Little Middle-Aged Girl: Rebecca's dad tries to persuade her to move back into the family home in San Diego. Woody and Kelly can't agree about where to live.
The Sigh: Debra's tired of sharing all her space with Ray, so he decides to give her exclusive use of their bathroom while he shares with the kids.
The Annoying Kid: Ray and Debra are excited to have finally met a couple who are just like them. But Ray begins to have mixed feelings about the couple's son.
She's the One: Robert's excited to be dating a great new woman, and hints that she might even be 'The One', but then Ray sees her do something strange...
Ski Lodge: Frasier's plans for a family holiday end in a romantic farce involving the Cranes, Daphne, her friend and a ski instructor.
Room Service: A surprise visit by Lilith stirs up powerful old feelings in Frasier and some surprising new ones in Niles.
Beware of Greeks: Frasier must confront an old family grudge with Aunt Zora when he runs into his cousin Nikos, who's getting married.
The Perfect Guy: When a seemingly perfect doctor joins the radio station as the host of a new show, Frasier desperately tries to find his flaw.
Iberville: The Nightmare Continues: Gordon's still helping Iberville Cuisine, in New Orleans' French Quarter. Will the staff walk out and implement their plan B?
Channel 4's midday news summary.

programmes.show_name: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programmes.show_description: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien meet mechanic Adrian in Worcestershire. Simon restores a wrecked armchair into punk rock furniture.

programmes.show_name: Matt Baker: Our Farm in the Dales
programmes.show_description: Matt calls in a family friend to help build a brand-new road around the farm, and Matt and Nicola head to Eryri (Snowdonia) to collect 10 specially selected ewes.
Colin Murray, Rachel Riley and Susie Dent host, with Nicki Chapman in Dictionary Corner, as contestants race against the clock to pit their wits against vowels, consonants and numbers.
Sara Damergi helps Bernie and Vernon find a holiday home on Fuerteventura, even though Vernon's never been there! Can they find the perfect place for their £150k?

programmes.show_name: A New Life in the Sun
programmes.show_description: Seasonal deadlines crank up the heat for Aggi and Camille in France and Angie and Ash in Italy. In Portugal, summer strain also bites for Harriet and Mondo's holiday cottages.
The new run continues. Visit two is to twins Sara and Paula's hotel in Devon. After an afternoon on the lash at a local vineyard, are there sour grapes in the feedback?
The new run continues. On a spectacular second night in Bedfordshire and Cambridgeshire, Dave serves up a menu melding flavours 'that no one else would ever think of combining'... (Ep2/5)

programmes.show_name: New: A Place in the Sun: What...
programmes.show_description: ...Happened Next?: Ben Hillman learns how Nicola and Paul turned their Lanzarote 'happy place' into a sleek home. And Leah Charles-King is on the Costa del Sol with Kim and Debbie.
Channel 4's flagship news programme. Including the weather.
The Labour Party: Ken Skates MS, Member of the Senedd for Clwyd South, sets out Welsh Labour's plans to improve the transport network in Wales.
It's double trouble for the Bodleys, as cockapoo twins Milo and Mabel burst into the pen. Can canine and family energy levels match? And Floyd the stray lurcher needs a good feed.
It's the final. With a race across Britain and a £100k prize at stake, how do the pairs work together? Do they bond? Or do tensions boil over? And who will be Handcuffed champions? (Ep6)
On the 'Box this time: The Assembly, Genius Game, The Piano, Scam Interceptors, The Americas, Married at First Sight Australia and BBC News (noisy phones).
The Capture, Married at First Sight Australia and Louis Theroux: Inside the Manosphere are some of the shows on this time. And there's also Will and Ralf Should Know Better.
...Experiment: Natalie and Tom are left questioning everything after some steamy intimacy with Josh and Holly, while Chanice and Richardo invite 15 of the residents for an orgy.
The naked truth on sex camming with the intimate stories of different women building empires from their bedrooms - like Maya, who quit her medical degree to go on camera full-time. (Ep1/2)
Café Kingston: In California, Gordon visits Jamaican restaurant Café Kingston. He meets micromanaging owner Dr Una Morris, who's also a radiotherapist.
Richard Ayoade and Michelle Wolf join Adam Hills, Josh Widdicombe and Alex Brooker to pick over the week's news and the latest action from the Milano Cortina Winter Paralympics.
Jamie Oliver makes a sublime dukkah roast lamb feast with a fantastic hack to speed up cooking.
Stacey helps Jo and Darryl, whose seaside dream in Devon has turned into a nightmare. Can Stacey turn the tide and help them realise their dream of an upside-down living space? (Ep2/6)
Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer catch up with Sarah and Ian, who had to house-hunt beyond Balham in south London, and Louise and Matt, who weren't sure about moving north of the Thames.
Another chance to catch yesterday's new episode, with Nicki Chapman in Dictionary Corner. Colin Murray, Rachel Riley and Susie Dent host.
Love Me, Love My Car: When Sam hears that the friend who bought his beloved car has died, he plots and schemes to get it back. Rebecca takes to Woody and Kelly's pet pig.
Sunday Dinner: Cliff and Norm are hired to film a family celebration at the bar. Frasier's invited to Sunday dinner with Shauna, his temporary secretary.
Norm's Big Audit: Sam panics when the gang try to watch one of his old baseball games. Norm flirts with a tax authority official to avoid an audit.
Marie's Vision: Marie visits the optician and winds up with a pair of glasses just in time for the holidays, but the family feel on edge when she starts seeing them clearly.
The Thought That Counts: After Ray lies and tells Debra that he's bought her an incredibly thoughtful Christmas present, he frantically tries to figure out what to get her.
Grandpa Steals: Frank must find a way to improve his image with Ally when she catches him 'sampling' some trail mix at the supermarket.
Bad Dog: Frasier can't handle Bulldog's five seconds of fame when he takes the credit for saving Roz during a hold-up at Café Nervosa.
Frasier's Gotta Have It: When Frasier begins dating a beautiful woman, he has trouble accepting that the relationship is largely built on physical attraction.
First Date: Niles plans a fake first date so that Daphne won't find out how he feels about her.
Roz and the Schnoz: Roz meets the parents of her baby's father and is shocked to discover a family facial characteristic that simply can't be ignored.
Voleo's: Gordon tries to revamp Voleo's Seafood Restaurant, a family-run restaurant in Westwego, Louisiana, that's been struggling since Hurricane Ida in 2021.
Channel 4's midday news summary.

programmes.show_name: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programmes.show_description: Simon O'Brien and Max McMurdo head to Surrey to meet upcycler Rob, who has a range of sheds a few miles from Gatwick Airport.

programmes.show_name: Matt Baker: Our Farm in the Dales
programmes.show_description: The Baker family's efforts begin to pay off, as the farm and its inhabitants are given a new lease of life. And Matt reveals his plans to increase biodiversity in the woodland.
Colin Murray, Rachel Riley and Susie Dent host, with Nicki Chapman in Dictionary Corner, as contestants race against the clock to pit their wits against vowels, consonants and numbers.
Trish and Ruth love Lanzarote so much that they want to buy a holiday home there, near the popular resort of Puerto del Carmen. Sara Damergi shows them what their £140k can buy.

programmes.show_name: A New Life in the Sun
programmes.show_description: In Italy, there's a tight turnaround for Angie and Ash. In Portugal, Harriet and Mondo branch out into events. And in Spain, Rachael and Lee bid to earn a crust at a pie shop.
The new run continues. Delightful décor, stunning views, sumptuous sausages and proper milk await the B&Bers at Simon and Vanessa's coastal guesthouse in Weymouth.
The new run continues. On night three in Bedfordshire and Cambridgeshire, Martina prepares Thai curries and poached pears. But one of her guests doesn't like spice or fruit. (Ep3/5)

programmes.show_name: New: A Place in the Sun: What...
programmes.show_description: ...Happened Next?: Danni Menzies returns to Dénia to check in on Alison, Brian and their dogs. Jasmine Harman heads back to sun-kissed Kapparis for a catch-up with Bill and Sandra.
Channel 4's flagship news programme. Including the weather.
The Conservative Party: Claire Coutinho MP, Shadow Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, sets out the Conservative Party's Cheap Power Plan.

programmes.show_name: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or...
programmes.show_description: ...List It: Lexie and Ed's four-bed, one-bath Nottingham home is too small for their family of six. Ed wants to move but Lexie loves the location. Who can help, Kirstie or Phil?
Epic rags-to-riches drama with Brenda Blethyn and Jessica Reynolds. Will Emma take Mac's offer to leave Fairley - and Edwin - for good? Adele's sobriety re-ignites her marriage. (Ep3/8)

programmes.show_name: The Push: Murder on the Cliff
programmes.show_description: True crime documentary. In 2021, a young lawyer fell from the top of Arthur's Seat in Edinburgh. Her husband was soon arrested for murder. As his trial begins, dark secrets emerge. (Ep1/2)

programmes.show_name: The Push: Murder on the Cliff
programmes.show_description: Did young lawyer Fawziyah Javed fall to her death or was she pushed? At her husband's murder trial, the jury are told about a dying declaration before returning their verdict. (Ep2/2)
